metadata.title: pyGCodeDecode
metadata.description: For the analysis of GCode, slicers or dedicated analyzer tools, such as Prusa Slicer or  gCodeViewer, merely display target process parameters. The actual process velocity however is  determined by the firmware of the printer. Velocities are expected to deviate significantly from  the target velocity at times of acceleration and deceleration. pyGCodeDecode aims to model the  machine's actual behavior and visualize its influence.

metadata.notes: This package reads the target trajectory firmware settings changes from a GCode file. Subsequently, it simulates a motion planner with acceleration and jerk or junction control respectively. The more accurate modeling is achieved by replicating grbl's and its derivatives' firmware-specific movement planner solutions, such as "Classic Jerk" and "Junction Deviation", as an interpretation for Jerk. The simulation result is a description of the nozzle and extrusion axis position and velocity over time and is easily accessible for further analysis. For example it can be used to generate time dependent boundary conditions, needed in additive manufacturing simulations for the Fused Filament Fabrication (FFF) process. The package includes 3D plotting functions, so it can be used to visualize local velocities before printing to improve your process understanding.

metadata.title: Impact Analysis of Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Urban) in Chhattisgarh: A Division-Wise Performance Evaluation
metadata.description: Tamrakar, Dr. Anand, Sinha, Harshika, Rao, Pragati (2025): Impact Analysis of Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Urban) in Chhattisgarh: A Division-Wise Performance Evaluation. FishTaxa 36 (9): 448-453
references: Beyond the physical aspect, housing is one of the most basic human needs, which is essential for social security, economic stability, and a better quality of life. It is also part of the core elements of sustainable development in both urban and rural settings. The rapid growth of the population, migration, and increasing costs of land have increased the demand for affordable housing in Indian urban cities, especially among the economically weaker sections and low-income groups. The Government of India, to meet the increasing demand, launched the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U) in 2015, based on the concept of "Housing for All." The aim of the program is to make sure that each eligible urban household has a pucca house by providing financial assistance in the form of subsidies and credit-linked loans. PMAY-U operates through four main components: In-Situ Slum Redevelopment (ISSR), the Credit-Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS), Affordable Housing in Partnership (AHP), and Beneficiary-Led Construction (BLC). Each component addresses distinctive beneficiary needs and urban housing situations, hence making the scheme applicable across different socio-economic settings.

data.title: Impact Analysis of Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Urban) in Chhattisgarh: A Division-Wise Performance Evaluation

metadata.title: Clifti-GPT benchmark AnnData: reference and query splits for federated single-cell experiments
metadata.description:
Clifti-GPT benchmark AnnData

Prepared reference and query AnnData (.h5ad) files used to reproduce Clifti-GPT experiments across six public single-cell cohorts. Each archive contains the two files required by the experiment registry in the Clifti-GPT repository. Gene expression is filtered to the scGPT vocabulary; highly variable genes (HVG) are not used for subsetting.

Related deposit (init weights, separate record): 10.5281/zenodo.20489646

This deposit provides the minimal benchmark bundle for reproducing Clifti-GPT fine-tuning, annotation, and embedding experiments. Files are split into reference (labeled training) and query (held-out test) AnnData objects per cohort, matching the layout expected under data/scgpt/benchmark/ in the Clifti-GPT codebase.

File

Cohort

Contents inside zip

ms.zip

Multiple Sclerosis

ms/reference_annot.h5ad, ms/query_annot.h5ad

hp5.zip

Human Pancreas

hp5/reference.h5ad, hp5/query.h5ad

lung.zip

Lung-Kim

lung/reference_annot.h5ad, lung/query_annot.h5ad

cl.zip

Cell line

cl/reference.h5ad, cl/query.h5ad

covid.zip

COVID-19 (uncorrected)

covid/reference-raw.h5ad, covid/query-raw.h5ad

myeloid.zip

Myeloid (base)

myeloid/reference_adata.h5ad, myeloid/query_adata.h5ad

metadata.title: Analysis code for: "Occupational Thermal Stress and Clustering of Injury Event in the Construction Industry: Integration of Environmental and Sociodemographic Factors in Brazil
metadata.description:
This archive contains the Python code used to perform the statistical analysis and produce the results for the research article "Occupational Thermal Stress and Clustering of Injury Event in the Construction Industry: Integration of Environmental and Sociodemographic Factors in Brazil".

The code was developed to analyze the dataset available at https://zenodo.org/records/17458229, which integrates work accident notifications from the Brazilian Notifiable Diseases Information System (SINAN) with meteorological data from the National Institute of Meteorology (INMET), covering the period from 2006 to 2024.

The analysis includes the following steps:

Preprocessing of numerical and categorical variables (imputation of missing values using SimpleImputer with median strategy, retention of 'unknown' category). Calculation of multiple thermal stress indices (WBGT, Heat Index, Humidex, ET, ETW, DI, MDI, Apparent Temperature, and WindChill). Dimensionality reduction using Factor Analysis of Mixed Data (FAMD)

Clustering using k-means algorithm with validation via elbow method and Silhouette coefficient. Statistical comparisons between clusters (Kolmogorov-Smirnov, Kruskal-Wallis, Dunn's post hoc test, and chi-square test). Generation of all tables and figures presented in the article.

The code was written in Python (version 3.10.0) and relies on the following libraries: Pandas (v2.2.2), NumPy (v2.1.3), Matplotlib (v3.103.0), Seaborn (v0.12.2), scikit-learn (v1.6.1), PRINCE (v0.16.1), and kmodes (v0.12.2). The code is provided as Jupyter notebooks (.ipynb) and can be executed sequentially to reproduce all results.
keywords: occupational heat stress; construction industry; cluster analysis; k-means; Brazil

data.title: Analysis code for: "Occupational Thermal Stress and Clustering of Injury Event in the Construction Industry: Integration of Environmental and Sociodemographic Factors in Brazil
